formatted_address: 21106 Kenswick Meadows Ct, Humble, TX 77338, USA
pitch_rationale: The subject property (Humble, TX) is under active construction with lumber and framing materials visible. The completed upper hip-roof section shows moderate shadow depth relative to eave width and a ridge noticeably narrower than the building footprint but not dramatically so, consistent with a 6:12 pitch. All surrounding fully-built homes display near-identical hip roofs with matching shadow lengths and ridge-to-eave ratios, confirming a neighborhood norm of ~6:12. The southern Houston-area climate further supports this mid-range pitch. Confidence is reduced due to the partially unfinished state of the roof obscuring full geometry assessment.
footprint_rationale: The subject home (centered on the orange reticle, bounded by the orange dashed box) appears to be under active construction. The bounding box measures approximately 255 × 268 pixels (54.1 ft × 56.8 ft), yielding a plan-view bounding area of ~3,074 sq ft. The roof is a complex hip style with multiple planes visible; the upper-left portion shows a completed darker hip section with discernible ridge/hip lines, while the lower-central area shows lighter exposed decking consistent with active framing. A fill factor of ~78% was applied to account for the hip corners tapering inward. No attached garage or separate structure is clearly connected within the box. Ridge (~2 segments, ~35 ft), hip lines (~6 edges from peaks to corners, ~95 ft total), valleys (~2 inward junctions, ~18 ft), no rakes (hip roof), and perimeter eaves (~155 ft) were estimated by tracing visible roof-plane edges in pixel coordinates and converting at 0.212 ft/px. Confidence is reduced due to the construction state partially obscuring roof structure and the unusual orientation of the home relative to north.
fence_reason: vision roof area 2683 differs from solar 2389 by 12.3% (>12%)
